title: Agentic Engineering Use Cases
summary: Representative ways to position the model across enterprise software modernization.

items.title: Legacy modernization
items.problem: Large legacy systems are costly to change, difficult to understand, and constrained by undocumented dependencies.
items.approach: Use DDD and event storming to rediscover the domain, apply modernization patterns, and introduce governed human-agent delivery across design, build, release, and operations.
outcomes: Transferable operating model for future waves

items.title: Regulated software delivery
items.problem: Teams want AI-enabled delivery, but risk, audit, and compliance requirements limit uncontrolled autonomy.
items.approach: Apply the governance model to define risk tiers, human checkpoints, evidence packs, and policy-based controls while using agents in lower-risk, high-volume engineering work.
outcomes: Reduced friction between delivery and control functions

items.title: Platform-enabled modernization
items.problem: Modernization programs stall because each team assembles its own tooling, patterns, and prompts with little reuse.
items.approach: Establish an internal developer platform with reusable workflows, context assets, templates, agent guardrails, and runbooks aligned to the operating model.
